steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content: Angles $U$ and $V$ add to $90$ degrees, so they are complementary. The sine of one acute angle equals the cosine of its complement.

steps.title: Apply the identity
steps.content: Use the cofunction relationship. $$ \sin(U)=\cos(V) $$ Since $$ \sin(U)=\frac{6\sqrt{2}}{11} $$ it follows that $$ \cos(V)=\frac{6\sqrt{2}}{11} $$ The answer is A.
